cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure out this question. So, the passage is about the steps taken when looking for a missing child. The question is asking which option is correct based on the passage. First, let me read through the passage again to understand the key points. The passage mentions that the first thing they do is put an APB (which I know is an "All-Points Bulletin") to all police stations. Then they call hospitals, check with parents, friends, relatives, follow movements, check local and national papers, put up posters, go on TV, and in America there's a magazine with photos of missing children. However, they also mention that with nearly two million missing children every year, they can't do all these things for everyone because of time, money, and lack of personnel. Now, looking at the options: A) can always find him – The passage doesn't say they can always find the child. In fact, it implies that there are limitations. So A is probably not correct. B) couldn't always try every way mentioned above – The passage says "we can't do all these things for everyone" due to time, money, and personnel. So B seems correct because they can't always use all the methods for every case. C) just try one way or another – The passage lists several methods they use, not just one. So C is probably incorrect.
